seo.title: Wyoming Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Wyoming — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license wyoming, dental assistant certification wyoming, RDA wyoming, DANB CDA wyoming, dental assistant radiography wyoming

license_types.title: Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Wyoming.

steps.title: Review Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Confirm you meet the eligibility criteria for an Expanded Functions Dental Assistant (EFDA) registration. This includes possessing an active CPR certification, completing a board-approved EFDA training program (minimum 60 hours with a clinical component), and passing an EFDA written and clinical examination administered by the Western Regional Examining Board (WREB) or the Dental Assisting National Board (DANB).

steps.title: Download Application Form
steps.description: Download and print the 'Expanded Functions Dental Assistant (EFDA) Application' form directly from the Wyoming Board of Dental Examiners EFDA Application page. Complete the form accurately and entirely.

steps.title: Gather Supporting Documents
steps.description: Collect all required supporting documents as specified in the application packet. These typically include a copy of your active CPR certification, an original or certified copy of your EFDA training program certificate, official proof of passing your EFDA examination (WREB or DANB), and a photocopy of your current photo identification.

steps.title: Prepare Application Fee
steps.description: Prepare a payment of $25.00 for the application fee. Ensure the payment method complies with the instructions on the application form (e.g., check or money order made payable to the Wyoming Board of Dental Examiners).

steps.title: Mail Complete Application
steps.description: Assemble the completed application form, all supporting documents, and the application fee, and mail the entire package to the Wyoming Board of Dental Examiners at the address provided on the application form.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: After submission, allow time for the Board to process your application. You can use the Board's online License Lookup tool to verify your registration status once it has been issued.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Dental Assistant through the Wyoming Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

faqs.question: Does Wyoming require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Wyoming requires Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Wyoming?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Wyoming.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Wyoming?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Wyoming?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $50. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Dental Assistant through the Wyoming Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Wyoming
hero.dek: In Wyoming, general dental assistants are not licensed. To register as an Expanded Functions Dental Assistant (EFDA), download the application form from the Wyoming Board of Dental Examiners EFDA Application page and submit it via mail.
